protocol_refs.title: NASEMSO National Model EMS Clinical Guidelines v3.0 — Head Injury
Michigan Head and Spine Trauma / Head Injury TBI Protocol

checklist_items.description: Neurological assessment performed — formal GCS with subscores plus LOC and vomiting history
checklist_items.done_feedback: Neurological assessment performed — formal GCS with E/V/M subscores and LOC/vomiting history documented.
checklist_items.missed_feedback: Complete neurological assessment not performed — AVPU or confusion alone is not a formal head-injury neuro package. Calculate GCS with E/V/M subscores and obtain LOC plus vomiting history.
common_errors: Documenting only the GCS total without eye, verbal, and motor components.

checklist_items.description: Pupil assessment performed — pupils checked bilaterally for size, equality, and reactivity
checklist_items.done_feedback: Pupils assessed bilaterally — size, equality, and reactivity documented for TBI trend monitoring.
checklist_items.missed_feedback: Pupil assessment missing — a head-injured patient needs bilateral pupil size, equality, and reactivity documented because asymmetry can indicate rising ICP.

checklist_items.done_feedback: Focused head DCAP-BTLS assessment performed — scalp/skull tenderness, deformity, and step-offs documented.
checklist_items.missed_feedback: Focused head DCAP-BTLS assessment not performed — scalp lacerations, tenderness, swelling, and skull step-offs are structural findings that change triage and neuro concern.
common_errors: Assessing mental status only and skipping the scalp/skull exam.

checklist_items.description: Spinal motion restriction applied when mechanism and neurological status indicate it
checklist_items.done_feedback: Spinal motion restriction applied when indicated by mechanism and neurological status.
checklist_items.missed_feedback: SMR not applied — head injury with concerning mechanism or altered mental status requires spinal precautions according to local protocol.
common_errors: Ruling out SMR using midline tenderness alone in an altered patient.

checklist_items.description: Priority transport initiated for head injury with altered mental status, vomiting, or focal neurological concern
checklist_items.done_feedback: Priority transport initiated — head injury with neurological concern requires rapid ED/trauma-center evaluation.
checklist_items.missed_feedback: Transport decision not made or delayed — head injury with altered mental status, vomiting, or focal neurological findings requires urgent transport.
common_errors: Extending scene time for a detailed exam after identifying concerning head-injury signs.

checklist_items.description: No ALS-only interventions attempted by BLS provider
checklist_items.done_feedback: ALS-only interventions correctly avoided — BLS head injury management is airway, oxygenation, SMR, and transport.
checklist_items.missed_feedback: ALS-only interventions attempted — IV access, advanced airway, and medications are outside BLS scope for isolated head injury.
common_errors: Attempting IV/IO access or medication administration at BLS level.

checklist_items.description: SMR applied according to local head/spine trauma protocol when indicated
checklist_items.done_feedback: SMR applied per protocol — mechanism and neurological status support spinal precautions.
checklist_items.missed_feedback: SMR not applied per protocol — concerning head-injury mechanism or altered LOC requires spinal precautions according to the active protocol.
common_errors: Waiting for neck pain or midline tenderness before applying SMR to an altered head-injury patient.

checklist_items.description: Continuous high-flow O2 via NRB applied for suspected TBI/head injury with altered mental status
checklist_items.done_feedback: High-flow O2 via NRB applied — protocol-aligned oxygenation support for suspected TBI/head injury with altered mental status.
checklist_items.missed_feedback: High-flow O2 via NRB not documented — head-injury protocols call for preventing hypoxia and maintaining oxygenation in suspected TBI.
common_errors: Relying on normal room-air SpO2 alone in a suspected TBI patient with altered mental status.
